content:
✅ Approved the contribution.
Comment: Helps others learn how to use programming to solve word problems/apply it to real-life scenarios. It really tests one's critical thinking over one's ability to program, and I really respect those kinds of clashes. Nice job!

✅ Approved the contribution.
Comment: It was clear to understand, and I think it's a relevant/funny topic. If I had this in a clash of code I would enjoy it

✅ Approved the contribution.
Comment: Nice puzzle, very short but it requires you to focus and think about what you're doing :>

Formatting
* Please study the formatting syntax on this page: https://www.codingame.com/playgrounds/40701/help-center/statement , and format your statement properly as described in the points below.

Input
* Please format all line numbers in <<bold>>, e.g. "<<Line 1>>".

* Please format all [[variables]], e.g. [[tokensPerRequest]].

Output
* Lines 2 & 3: Please format "X" as a [[variable]].

* Line 2 only: Please format "At this rate you will reach the maximum quota" and "days" as {{constants}}.

* Line 5: Please format "You will not reach the quota at this pace" as a {{constant}}.

Constraints
* Please format all [[variables]].

Goal / Output
* Reaching the quota and exceeding the quota are not the same. Please replace "exceed" with "reach" in both sections.

Cases
* Please reorder/modify the cases so that each test and its validator represent the same scenario with slightly different inputs and outputs (or the same output where the result is "not reaching the quota").

* Ensure that the test cases collectively cover the key scenarios below.
  - Clear-cut within quota: usage remains safely below the quota even after all remaining days, no rounding down is needed.
  - Clear-cut out of quota: quota will be reached well before the end of the month, no rounding down is needed.
  - The quota would be exceeded during a day, rounding down is needed.
  - UsedTokens == maxTokens
  - Daily usage larger than remaining quota (quota would be reached within the first day).
  - Number of days remaining equals the number of days until quota is reached.
  
  If any of the above scenarios are not covered by the existing cases, please add additional pairs of cases to ensure the scenarios are tested.

Thanks.

Thank you for following up. A few remaining matters require attention:

Constraints
* Please remove "All values are integers."

Cases
* Please remove the trailing newlines from the output of all the cases.

* Some tests aren't yet aligned with their corresponding validators. Please update the cases as follows:

  • Test 1: Please reduce [[maxTokens]] by a positive multiple of 32000 so that [[days_left]] > [[days_to_limit]] > 0.

  • Validator 3: Please modify the case such that [[max_tokens]] = [[used_tokens]], similar to Test 3.

* Some scenarios haven't been covered by any cases yet. Without modifying the existing cases, please add these cases. All input values in the new cases should be distinct from the existing cases and among themselves.

  • Test 5 and Validator 5: Similar to Test 1 and Validator 1, but [[remaining]] is not a multiple of [[daily_usage]], and [[days_left]] > [[days_to_limit]] > 0.

  • Test 6 and Validator 6: [[max_tokens]] > [[used_tokens]] but [[max_tokens]] < [[used_tokens]] + [[daily_usage]].

Thanks.

Output
* Please format "X" (in "X rounded down") as a [[variable]].

Cases
* Validator 3 violates the final constraint. Please change [[daysLeft]] to a positive integer.

* Please add Test 7 and Validator 7: [[remaining]] is not a multiple of [[daily_usage]], and [[days_left]] > [[days_to_limit]] > 1.

(In Test 5 and Validator 5, [[days_left]] < [[days_to_limit]], and that's not what was requested, but let's just keep those cases.)

Thanks.
